evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` lives in shared lib but directly imports UI toast (`@/shared/components/ui/toast`), so non-UI hooks (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`) inherit presentation side effects via a core error helper.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` exports `supabase` as a module-level singleton that triggers instrumentation install, runtime init, and `window` global registration on first import, creating implicit startup coupling and order-sensitive behavior.

`src/shared/hooks/useMobile.tsx` centralizes UA sniffing, pointer heuristics, orientation, and layout policy (`mobileColumns`) in one shared hook; downstream components (`src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx`, media gallery/lightbox flows) couple to this single policy hub.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx` combines modal composition, z-index arbitration, background-dismiss policy, touch/pointer filtering, iOS viewport bug repair, and tasks-pane layout offset logic in one component, indicating mixed ownership.

`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` includes both primitive wrappers and behavior-rich `TouchableTooltip`, while feature code like `src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx` re-implements separate mobile tooltip state/control, signaling unclear responsibility boundaries for interaction policy.

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx` defines app/theme-specific variants (`retro`, `theme-*`, `success`) in a nominally shared primitive, and `src/shared/components/MediaTypeFilter.tsx` depends on those variant contracts (`retro`, `retro-dark`), tightening cross-feature styling coupling in the shared layer.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:250` and `src/shared/lib/tasks/zImageTurboI2I.ts:201` duplicate the same batch pattern (validate -> expand param bag -> call single-task helper with Promise.allSettled).
`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts:446` repeats the same orchestration shape with another large task-param object and parallel dispatch.
`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:231` builds a very wide adapter object (`buildImageEditValueParams`) that mostly forwards fields/functions from sub-hooks.
`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/hooks/useApplySettingsHandler.ts:116` maps a large context object into another large context object with near 1:1 property forwarding.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/hooks/useApplySettingsHandler.ts:211` coordinates DB fetch, invalidation, sleeps, position reloads, and many service calls in one seam, making ordering assumptions part of behavior.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/hooks/useApplySettingsHandler.ts:192` and `:264` rely on timed waits (`setTimeout` via Promise delay) to synchronize state propagation across modules.

`src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts:94` uses throttled batch preloading with optional full task prefetch while `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:209` separately owns pagination/query freshness, increasing cross-module choreography complexity.
`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:14` defines an oversized parameter interface and then re-slices it into multiple pass-through builders, increasing local ceremony.
`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useImageEditValue.ts:130`, `:203`, and `:205` inject no-op setters (`() => {}`), which makes local state contracts less honest and harder to reason about.
`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:400` manually rebuilds a large nested return object from many sources, creating brittle field-by-field mapping overhead.

`ShotImagesEditorEditActions` uses `targetPosition` for batch drops while sibling shot-editor contracts document/use `targetFrame` (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/types.ts:104`, `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/ShotSettingsContext.tsx:74`).

`buildTravelRequestBody` accepts two overlapping input shapes (nested `motionParams/generationParams/seedParams` plus legacy flat fields), creating ambiguous precedence and a wide boundary (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:461`).

`getUserWithTimeout` exposes a timeout-oriented name but is a synchronous cache/storage read wrapped in `Promise.resolve`, which makes call-site expectations about cancellation/latency misleading (`src/shared/lib/toolSettingsService.ts:84`).

`InvalidationOptions.reason`/`VariantInvalidationOptions.reason` are required but never read in implementation, so the public option contract advertises behavior that does not exist (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:7`, `src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts:24`).
`src/shared/hooks/usePromptFieldState.ts` contains large repeated prose blocks (priority logic + long example) that restate the implementation branches rather than adding boundary context.

`src/shared/hooks/useTaskPlaceholder.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` include tutorial-style doc comments/examples around small hooks, creating high comment-to-logic ratio and maintenance noise.
`src/shared/hooks/useShots.ts` is a one-line pass-through export wrapped in explanatory banner text, indicating ceremony around trivial glue code.
`src/shared/hooks/useShots.ts` and `src/shared/hooks/shots/index.ts` maintain dual-path compatibility for shot hooks (top-level + modular barrel), signaling an unfinished import-path migration.
`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/types.ts` keeps multiple `@deprecated` fields directly in active `ProjectImageSettings`, so old and new settings schemas coexist in the primary interface.
`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` preserves many deprecated task fields in shared contracts, extending legacy payload shape into current type surfaces.

`src/shared/hooks/` is a flat package with 97 production files (directory_profiles.file_count=97), well above the mixed-concern split threshold (>10), and this batch’s assigned hooks span unrelated domains (`useAIInteractionService.ts`, `useAdjacentPagePreloader.ts`, `useApiKeys.ts`).

`src/shared/components/` is also overloaded (directory_profiles.file_count=80) and mixes production and test files in the same folder (zones: production=60, test=20); assigned files include co-located tests `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx` and `AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx` despite a parallel `src/shared/components/__tests__/` package existing.

Coupling evidence shows heavy broad shared dependencies (`src/shared/components/ → src/shared/hooks/` = 59 edges, `src/shared/hooks/ → src/shared/lib/` = 62, `src/shared/hooks/ → src/shared/lib/errorHandling/` = 44), reinforcing that these large flat folders act as catch-all boundaries rather than focused packages.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` defines a very wide composed props contract (core/navigation/shot/layout/button/media) and returns many grouped sub-facades, indicating one hook coordinating too many concerns.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` mixes persistence sync, inpainting/magic/reposition/img2img orchestration, and giant context assembly with placeholder state (`isFlippedHorizontally`, `isSaving`) inside one unit.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoShotDisplay.tsx` combines list-item rendering, shot rename/delete/duplicate mutations, localStorage confirmation policy, custom-event dispatch/listening, and final-video lightbox control in one 861-line component.

Timeline SQL behavior is repeatedly redefined across adjacent migrations (`supabase/migrations/20250922134600_fix_apply_timeline_frames_ambiguous_column_v2.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20250922134700_force_recreate_apply_timeline_frames.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20250922135100_create_apply_timeline_frames_v2.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20250922135800_create_atomic_timeline_update_final.sql`) with near-duplicated locking/validation/update stages, signaling unstable design boundaries.

`src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, and `src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx` use double quotes/no semicolons, while neighboring non-ui files in this batch (for example `src/app/routes.tsx`, `src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts`, `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`) consistently use single quotes/semicolons.
The style split is directory-local rather than language- or layer-driven, creating a predictable 'style island' when moving between `shared/components/ui` and other shared/app code.
convention_outlier.unreported_risk: Only a subset of the codebase was sampled for this batch; there may be additional convention islands outside these files.

evidence: `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/hooks/useApplySettingsHandler.ts` mixes `console.error` + `toast.error` with manual returns instead of the shared `handleError` path used broadly elsewhere.

`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts` uses `handleError` in some branches but raw `console.error` + silent return in `handleVisitShotFromNotifier`, producing different failure behavior in the same hook.

`src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/MyLorasTab/hooks/useLoraSubmission.ts` uses direct `toast.error`/`toast.success` in upload branches but `handleError` in catch blocks, so normalization/logging is inconsistent.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/hooks/useApplySettingsHandler.ts` returns `[]` on Supabase snapshot fetch errors (`fetchPairPromptSnapshot`) and continues applying settings, which hides upstream fetch failures and can apply partial state.
`src/app/routes.tsx` documents one auth authority (Layout/AuthContext) but also uses localStorage token presence (`hasStoredSession`) for route redirection decisions.
`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts` infers auth failure by substring matching error messages (`includes('401')`/`includes('unauthorized')`) rather than a shared typed auth error contract.
`src/pages/Home/hooks/useDiscordSignIn.ts` writes `oauthInProgress` directly to localStorage while auth flow is otherwise Supabase-driven, indicating multiple ad hoc auth-state signals.
authorization_consistency.unreported_risk: This batch is client-heavy and does not include backend policy/middleware files, so true end-to-end authorization enforcement could still diverge from client assumptions.

findings.summary: Core error helper couples shared logic to UI rendering side effects.
evidence: `handleError` imports and calls toast directly, even though it is a shared lib utility.
Feature hooks call `handleError` from non-UI logic paths, so error handling always carries UI coupling unless each call suppresses toasts.
findings.suggestion: Split error normalization/logging from notification delivery: keep a pure `reportError` in `shared/lib/errorHandling`, and add a UI adapter (for toast) in a presentation-layer module. Update calling hooks to opt into UI notifications explicitly.

findings.summary: Supabase singleton still performs runtime initialization at import access boundary.
evidence: `supabase` is exported as `getOrCreateSupabaseClient()` result, so first import path triggers instrumentation install, runtime initialization, and `window` global writes.
Behavior depends on when modules first touch this export, which keeps startup effects implicit rather than explicitly orchestrated.
findings.suggestion: Export a side-effect-free accessor (`getSupabaseClient`) and move initialization entry into an explicit app bootstrap path. Avoid writing globals except behind a dev-only diagnostics gate.

findings.summary: Lightbox shell mixes infrastructure, interaction, and layout ownership in one unit.
evidence: LightboxShell contains browser bug repair, scroll lock policy, touch filtering, z-index conflict checks, background-dismiss choreography, and pane-offset layout logic.
Mobile interaction logic also exists in gallery hooks/device hooks, creating scattered ownership of touch/pointer policy.
findings.suggestion: Decompose LightboxShell into focused collaborators: `useLightboxDismissal`, `useLightboxScrollLock`, and a pure layout wrapper. Keep device heuristics in one adapter consumed by both gallery and lightbox interaction layers.

findings.summary: Shared UI layer carries feature/theme-specific contracts that blur ownership.
evidence: Button primitive contains many app-brand variants and typography assumptions (`retro`, `theme-*`, custom fonts), making shared component semantics feature-specific.
MediaTypeFilter depends on those exact variant names, so feature components are coupled to shared primitive internals.
CreativePartnerPane bypasses shared button with raw `<button>` styling, suggesting mismatch between shared primitive responsibility and feature needs.
findings.suggestion: Separate a minimal base button from brand/theme wrappers (e.g., `BaseButton` + `ThemeButton` in feature/design-system layer). Update MediaTypeFilter to depend on stable semantic props rather than variant-name strings.

findings.summary: Task-creation modules repeat the same orchestration with wide param forwarding
evidence: `createBatchMagicEditTasks` (`magicEdit.ts:250`) and `createBatchZImageTurboI2ITasks` (`zImageTurboI2I.ts:201`) both expand batch params into per-task bags and call nearly identical single-task wrappers.
`createBatchImageGenerationTasks` (`imageGeneration.ts:446`) repeats the same shape with another large mapped parameter object.
This repeated scaffold indicates missing shared abstraction for batch task creation + per-task payload builder.
findings.suggestion: Extract a typed `createBatchTasks` utility in `src/shared/lib/tasks` that handles validation hook, per-item payload derivation, seed strategy, parallel execution, and `processBatchResults`; keep each task file focused on task-specific payload transformation only.

findings.summary: Apply-settings seam depends on timing and ordered side effects
evidence: `applySettingsFromTask` (`useApplySettingsHandler.ts:211`) mixes transport, cache invalidation, persistence, and UI synchronization in one boundary function.
Synchronization uses fixed delays (`useApplySettingsHandler.ts:192` and `:264`) before/after reload, which encodes temporal coupling instead of explicit readiness signals.
Task/query freshness concerns are split across different modules (`useTasks.ts` + `DataFreshnessManager.ts`), so this seam manually coordinates cross-module consistency.
findings.suggestion: Split apply-settings into an explicit pipeline (`fetch -> transform -> persist -> rehydrate`) with awaited completion signals from persistence steps; replace fixed sleeps with event/query completion checks, and centralize refresh orchestration in one utility consumed by both apply-settings and task hooks.

findings.summary: Inline edit state assembly is over-fragmented and uses contract-padding no-ops
evidence: `UseImageEditValueParams` (`useImageEditValue.ts:14`) is very large and then copied through multiple builder functions with mostly direct property forwarding.
`useImageEditValue.ts` sets placeholder callbacks (`setIsMagicEditMode`, `setQwenEditModel`, `setAdvancedSettings`) to no-ops, which hides unsupported actions behind a full interface.
`buildInlineEditStateResult` (`useInlineEditState.ts:400`) repeats extensive field mapping, increasing drift risk when any sub-hook contract changes.
findings.suggestion: Narrow `ImageEditState` to capability-specific slices and expose only supported actions; remove no-op API padding by introducing partial feature interfaces, and replace manual object reconstruction with direct grouped sub-state returns consumed by `InlineEditView`.

findings.summary: Batch drop APIs use conflicting insertion parameter names/semantics.
evidence: `ShotImagesEditorEditActions.onBatchFileDrop/onBatchGenerationDrop` are typed with `targetPosition`.
`ShotSettingsContext` explicitly documents unified `targetFrame` for both timeline and batch handlers.
findings.suggestion: Standardize batch-drop contracts on `targetFrame?: number` end-to-end and remove `targetPosition` from public interfaces; provide a temporary adapter type only at migration boundaries.

findings.summary: Spread-based prop flatteners erase ownership boundaries between config groups.
evidence: Both files export `resolve*Props` helpers that merge multiple groups into one large object via `...` spreads.
This pattern makes collisions silent and weakens seam clarity between display/image/edit/workflow and clip/motion/ui domains.
findings.suggestion: Keep grouped props at integration seams and pass structured objects through component boundaries; if flattening is necessary, enforce collision-safe namespaced keys and runtime asserts in one shared utility.

findings.summary: Travel request builder accepts overlapping modern and legacy inputs.
evidence: `buildTravelRequestBody` takes grouped params and also a parallel set of flat backward-compat fields in the same signature.
Function then computes fallback precedence across both sets, increasing ambiguity for callers and tests.
findings.suggestion: Split into two explicit builders (`buildTravelRequestBodyFromV2Params`, `buildTravelRequestBodyFromLegacyParams`) and keep compatibility conversion in a dedicated adapter layer.

findings.summary: Required `reason` option is not consumed by invalidation APIs.
evidence: `InvalidationOptions` and `VariantInvalidationOptions` require `reason`.
Neither `performInvalidation` nor `invalidateVariantChange` reads or logs `reason`, so the required field has no effect.
findings.suggestion: Either remove `reason` from the public type or wire it into structured telemetry/logging (and tests) so callers get real diagnostic value.

findings.summary: Join-clips duration retrieval is duplicated across two seam utilities.
evidence: `clipInitService` uses ad-hoc DOM `<video>` probing (`getVideoDurationFromUrl`).
`clipManager/metadata` uses shared `extractVideoMetadataFromUrl` with separate error and return behavior.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate on one duration/metadata service and reuse it in pending-clip ingestion and clip-manager updates to keep boundary behavior uniform.

findings.summary: Shot hooks migration remains incomplete due to active dual import entrypoints.
evidence: `useShots.ts` exports `export * from './shots'` as a top-level compatibility facade.
`shots/index.ts` explicitly documents legacy import compatibility instead of enforcing a single canonical path.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical import path for shot hooks, migrate call sites with a codemod, and keep a short-lived deprecated shim that emits lint warnings before removal.

findings.summary: Deprecated schema fields are still embedded in primary shared type contracts.
evidence: `ProjectImageSettings` includes a cluster of deprecated fields alongside current fields, so consumers can keep writing old shape.
`LegacyTravelBetweenImagesTaskParams` exposes many deprecated structure-video fields in shared task params, preserving dual-schema usage.
findings.suggestion: Move deprecated fields into explicit `Legacy*` types and normalize old payloads at boundary mappers; keep only normalized modern fields in primary interfaces.

findings.summary: Small hooks carry excessive explanatory boilerplate that duplicates code behavior.
evidence: `usePromptFieldState.ts` repeats branch logic in long top-of-file prose and inline comments for already explicit conditions.
`useTaskPlaceholder.ts` includes tutorial-style walkthrough text for a straightforward callback wrapper.
`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` includes detailed usage prose around a simple confirmation-state hook.
findings.suggestion: Trim comments to short intent/boundary notes, move longer behavioral explanation into tests/docs, and keep hook files focused on executable logic.

findings.summary: Utility and hook internals include imprecise cleanup/error branches.
evidence: `errorUtils.ts` duplicates cancellation classification with non-identical predicates, making behavior harder to predict.
`useServerForm.ts` cleanup invokes async save without explicit failure handling.
`useTaskPlaceholder.ts` unconditional refetch in `finally` runs even when creation fails or returns no task IDs.
findings.suggestion: Unify cancellation detection behind one helper used everywhere, make unmount save failure handling explicit (`void ...catch(...)` or queued persistence), and gate refetches on successful task resolution.

findings.summary: Unrelated hooks are accumulated in `src/shared/hooks/` instead of capability packages.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/` has 97 files (directory_profiles), indicating flat-directory overload.
This batch alone includes three different concerns (AI prompt orchestration, gallery page preloading, user API key persistence) in the same root hook folder.
Tests mirror the same root placement pattern (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/*`) instead of concern-based test boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create target folders `src/shared/hooks/ai/`, `src/shared/hooks/gallery/`, and `src/shared/hooks/auth/` (or `apiCredentials/`) with matching `__tests__` subfolders; 2) move files in order: `useAIInteractionService` + test to `ai`, then `useAdjacentPagePreloader` + test to `gallery`, then `useApiKeys` + test to `auth`; 3) update imports via `rg "useAIInteractionService|useAdjacentPagePreloader|useApiKeys" src` and adjust paths or add temporary barrel re-exports in old locations for one release; 4) validate with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run test -- useAIInteractionService useAdjacentPagePreloader useApiKeys`.

findings.summary: Component test placement is inconsistent between root co-location and dedicated test package.
evidence: Assigned component tests are in `src/shared/components/` root while the codebase also uses `src/shared/components/__tests__/` as a separate test package.
`src/shared/components/` has 80 files with mixed zones (60 production, 20 test), reducing top-level navigability.
This structural inconsistency blurs ownership expectations for where new tests should live.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) choose one test-placement rule for this package (recommended: move these tests into `src/shared/components/__tests__/` to match existing majority); 2) move `ActiveLoRAsDisplay.test.tsx` then `AppErrorBoundary.test.tsx`; 3) update local imports to point to `../ActiveLoRAsDisplay` and `../AppErrorBoundary`; 4) run `rg "ActiveLoRAsDisplay\.test|AppErrorBoundary\.test" src/shared/components` to confirm single canonical location and validate with `npm run test -- ActiveLoRAsDisplay AppErrorBoundary` plus `npm run typecheck`.

findings.summary: Lightbox hooks centralize too many responsibilities behind wide bag interfaces
evidence: `useSharedLightboxState` composes many feature hooks (variants/navigation/star/references/join/lineage/shot/source/layout/button) and requires a broad props surface split into multiple sub-interfaces.
`useImageEditOrchestrator` coordinates multiple edit systems plus persistence sync and builds a very large `imageEditValue` object with an extensive dependency list.
The parent `MediaLightbox` depends on these orchestration outputs, increasing change blast radius for unrelated edit/lightbox behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split orchestration by vertical feature boundaries (navigation+variants, shot actions, edit-mode engines) with narrower typed contracts; then compose at `MediaLightbox` via small adapters rather than one mega-hook return object.

findings.summary: VideoShotDisplay mixes mutation workflows, policy state, and rendering concerns
evidence: `VideoShotDisplay.tsx` handles rename/delete/duplicate mutations, localStorage skip-confirmation policy, app-event coordination, and visual mosaic/lightbox rendering in a single component.
The component carries many UI mode states (`isEditingName`, `isDeleteDialogOpen`, `isVideoModalOpen`, `showVideo`, `isFinalVideoLightboxOpen`, selection flags), making behavior coupling dense.
This item-level component becomes a control hub rather than a presentational leaf, making feature edits risky.
findings.suggestion: Extract a `useShotCardController` (commands + policy) and keep `VideoShotDisplay` view-focused; move persistence/event-policy concerns into reusable hooks/services consumed by list/page containers.

findings.summary: Desktop/mobile image managers implement divergent state machines for same feature
evidence: Container already constructs shared managers (`useOptimisticOrder`, `useSelection`, `useLightbox`, `useDragAndDrop`, `useBatchOperations`), but mobile component still owns separate optimistic/reconciliation and selection-control logic.
Desktop and mobile each carry their own transient success/selection/display timing flows, creating two behavioral implementations for one domain.
This split introduces inconsistent evolution paths between platforms when adding reorder/selection semantics.
findings.suggestion: Promote one canonical state machine in container hooks for optimistic ordering/selection actions and pass platform-specific render adapters only; migrate remaining mobile-local behavioral logic behind shared command hooks.

findings.summary: Timeline frame update design is repeatedly reimplemented across migrations
evidence: Adjacent migrations repeatedly drop/recreate or rename timeline update functions to reapply similar logic (lock, temp table, validation, staged update, position reconciliation).

Core algorithm is duplicated under multiple function names/versions (`apply_timeline_frames`, `apply_timeline_frames_v2`, `atomic_timeline_update`) rather than stabilized behind a single migration strategy.
Patch-on-patch replacement pattern suggests unresolved design boundary for timeline update semantics.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical timeline update function contract and migrate callers once; keep future changes as small parameterized deltas with regression tests, instead of re-cloning full function bodies under new names.

findings.summary: Shared `handleError` is bypassed by ad hoc `console.error`/`toast` paths in related flows.
evidence: `useApplySettingsHandler` logs with `console.error` and shows `toast.error` in catch branches instead of calling `handleError`.
`useMediaGalleryHandlers` uses `handleError` for some actions but uses `console.error` + return for missing shot branches.
`useLoraSubmission` emits direct `toast.error` for upload failures while other failures go through `handleError`.
findings.suggestion: Normalize all user-facing and operational error paths through `handleError` (with `showToast`/`toastTitle` options) and reserve raw `console.error` for explicitly temporary debug-only branches guarded by DEV checks.

findings.summary: Snapshot fetch failures are converted to empty data, masking real errors during apply-settings.
evidence: `fetchPairPromptSnapshot` catches Supabase errors and returns `[]` after `console.error`.
`applySettingsFromTask` continues with `preparePairPromptTargets([])` and subsequent apply steps, so upstream data failure is treated as a valid empty snapshot.
findings.suggestion: Return a typed failure (throw wrapped error via `handleError` context or `Result` object) and abort apply flow when snapshot fetch fails; only use empty arrays for confirmed no-data success cases.

findings.summary: UI primitives use a different formatting convention than adjacent shared/app modules.
evidence: UI primitive files in `shared/components/ui` use double quotes and omit semicolons.
Other files in the same batch (routing/hooks/task code) predominantly use single quotes with semicolons.
findings.suggestion: Decide and codify one convention boundary: either format `shared/components/ui/*` to match project defaults or document/enforce a scoped formatter override for vendor-style UI primitives so drift is intentional and machine-enforced.

findings.summary: Auth decisions rely on mixed localStorage/message heuristics instead of a single typed auth state source.
evidence: `routes.tsx` redirects using localStorage token presence (`hasStoredSession`) while comments state Layout/AuthContext is authoritative.
`useDiscordSignIn` sets `oauthInProgress` in localStorage separately from Supabase auth state.
`DataFreshnessManager` classifies `AUTH_ERROR` by string-matching generic error messages.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared auth-state utility (typed statuses from Supabase session events + explicit error codes) and migrate redirect/recovery logic to that source instead of localStorage/message heuristics.

findings.summary: Supabase singleton import still triggers broad runtime wiring across feature boundaries
evidence: `supabase` is exported from an immediate `getOrCreateSupabaseClient()` call in `client.ts`.
Runtime initialization bundles auth manager, reconnect, realtime instrumentation, and auto-login in one side-effect path.
Auth hooks consume the singleton directly, so feature imports transitively depend on infra boot behavior.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a two-phase API: `getSupabaseClient()` (pure client access) and explicit `startSupabaseRuntime()` called once from app bootstrap (`src/app/bootstrap.tsx`/`src/app/main.tsx`), then remove runtime wiring from client creation path.

findings.summary: Related flows mix raw throws, null returns, and toast-first handling
evidence: `invokeWithTimeout` throws raw `Error` values for timeout/http branches.
`useSharePageData` collapses fetch failure modes into a generic UI string and silent return.
Shot mutations use inline `toast.error` in some branches but `handleError` in others.
Generation task bridge both throws custom errors and separately calls `handleError` in hook wrappers.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one app-level error contract for async data paths (return `AppError`-typed failures or consistently normalize through `handleError`); add shared helpers for query/mutation error mapping and replace inline toasts with contract-driven presentation.

findings.summary: MediaLightbox abstraction adds heavy prop-bag translation overhead
evidence: Single component carries a large flat prop interface then repackages via `useBundledLightboxProps`.
Most logic is pass-through mapping into `ImageLightbox`/`VideoLightbox` rather than behavior reduction.
Extra wrapper export file adds another indirection layer without new contract value.
findings.suggestion: Split into explicit entry components (`ImageMediaLightbox`, `VideoMediaLightbox`) with narrower props and shared typed subcontracts; keep one small compatibility shim temporarily, then remove after call-sites migrate.

findings.summary: Tool metadata is maintained in parallel registries with drift risk
evidence: Both files define near-identical tool lists and must be updated in lockstep.
Back-compat re-export entry points keep multiple import surfaces for same API.
No single canonical source enforces coherence for tool settings/defaults/UI listing.
findings.suggestion: Create one canonical `toolsRegistry` module exporting typed records for settings/defaults/UI and derive both manifests from it; deprecate compatibility re-export modules with codemod-assisted import migration.

findings.summary: Routing uses token-presence checks while layout enforces real auth state
evidence: `authRedirectLoader` treats any localStorage token as authenticated intent.
`Layout` later performs authoritative auth check and redirects unauthenticated users.
`AuthGate` only blocks loading, so enforcement responsibility is split across loader/layout/gate.
findings.suggestion: Centralize auth gating into one authoritative route guard utility (session validity + expiry check) used by both router loaders and layout; keep `AuthGate` for loading-only concern and remove auth decision branching from route-local helpers.

findings.summary: Verbose debug scaffolding and ad hoc console usage reduce signal
evidence: Logger module contains broad interception scaffolding aimed at absorbing existing console volume.
Debug utilities include extensive operational narration and console output strings.
Feature modules still emit direct console errors rather than using unified logging/error channels.
findings.suggestion: Define structured log levels/events and route all production logs through a small logger API; move heavy debug helpers behind dev-only dynamic imports and remove direct console calls from production paths.

findings.summary: Legacy compatibility logic is still active in core runtime flows
evidence: Runtime invokes multiple migration hooks for legacy references/loras/scene modes.
Segment settings parser and metadata types support several deprecated shapes simultaneously.
Travel payload builder still strips legacy fields post-construction, showing ongoing dual-format handling.
findings.suggestion: Stage migration completion: (1) add telemetry counters for legacy field reads, (2) run one-time server/client cleanup to rewrite old records, (3) remove legacy branches and cleanup deletes once observed legacy usage reaches zero for a release window.

findings.summary: Directory structure is flattened around oversized shared packages
evidence: Structure profile: `src/shared/hooks/` has 97 files, `src/shared/components/` has 80, `src/shared/lib/` has 68.
Coupling matrix hotspots exceed threshold signal: `src/shared/components/ → src/shared/components/ui/` count 111 and `src/shared/hooks/ → src/shared/lib/` count 62.
Wrapper-shadow files duplicate module identities and obscure canonical package entry points.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/domains/{media,projects,tasks,auth}/` and `src/platform/{supabase,routing}`; Stage 2 move order: first pure utilities (`queryKeys`, task builders), then hooks by domain, then UI components by domain, then delete top-level wrapper shadows; Stage 3 consolidate registries into `src/tools/registry/`; import-update and validation commands: `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run`, then apply moves, then `rg -n "from '@/shared/(components|hooks|lib)' src`, `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm -s vitest run`.
